session.tool_result:
Exit code 1 ============================= test session starts ============================== platform linux -- Python 3.13.12, pytest-9.0.2, pluggy-1.6.0 rootdir: /home/runner/work/erk/erk configfile: pyproject.toml plugins: cov-7.0.0, xdist-3.8.0, anyio-4.12.1, asyncio-1.3.0 asyncio: mode=Mode.AUTO, debug=False, asyncio_default_fixture_loop_scope=function, asyncio_default_test_loop_scope=function collected 110 items
tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py .......F........... [ 17%] tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py ..............................F..... [ 50%] .........................................F............. [100%]
=================================== FAILURES =================================== __ TestExecuteCommandCopyCommands.test_copy_pr_checkout_script_copies_command __
self = <tests.tui.commands.test_execute_command.TestExecuteCommandCopyCommands object at 0x7feee4c0c770>
def test_copy_pr_checkout_script_copies_command(self) -> None: """copy_pr_checkout_script copies the PR checkout command with --script.""" row = make_pr_row(123, "Test") executor = FakeCommandExecutor() screen = PlanDetailScreen(row=row, executor=executor, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS) screen.execute_command("copy_pr_checkout_script") expected_cmd = 'source "$(erk pr checkout 123 --script)"' > assert executor.copied_texts == [expected_cmd] E assert ['source <(er...23 --script)'] == ['source "$(e...3 --script)"'] E E At index 0 diff: 'source <(erk pr checkout 123 --script)' != 'source "$(erk pr checkout 123 --script)"' E
... [68 lines omitted] ...
tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py:107: AssertionError tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py:318: AssertionError tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py:887: AssertionError FAILED tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py::TestExecuteCommandCopyCommands::test_copy_pr_checkout_script_copies_command - assert ['source <(er...23 --script)'] == ['source "$(e...3 --script)"'] FAILED tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py::test_display_name_copy_pr_checkout_script_shows_pr - assert 'source <(erk...831 --script)' == 'source "$(er...31 --script)"' FAILED tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py::test_get_copy_text_returns_display_name_for_valid_command - assert 'source <(erk...123 --script)' == 'source "$(er...23 --script)"' ======================== 3 failed, 107 passed in 0.45s =========================
session.assistant:
pytest failed: 3 issues found
Summary: - 107 passed, 3 failed in 0.45s - All failures are in copy_pr_checkout_script command tests - The issue is a string format mismatch in the checkout script syntax
Failures:
1. **tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py:107** - `test_copy_pr_checkout_script_copies_command` - Expected: `source "$(erk pr checkout 123 --script)"` - Got: `source <(erk pr checkout 123 --script)`
2. **tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py:318** - `test_display_name_copy_pr_checkout_script_shows_pr` - Expected: `source "$(erk pr checkout 5831 --script)"` - Got: `source <(erk pr checkout 5831 --script)`
3. **tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py:887** - `test_get_copy_text_returns_display_name_for_valid_command` - Expected: `source "$(erk pr checkout 123 --script)"` - Got: `source <(erk pr checkout 123 --script)`
The tests expect the command substitution syntax `"$(command)"` (standard POSIX shell) but the implementation is using `<(command)` (bash process substitution). This is a shell syntax difference - the tests need to be updated or the implementation code needs to change the format back to the expected syntax.
